CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-24-2025 Continued To Jul-02-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss The Complaint For Forum Non Conveniens Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Stay
Off Calendar May-08-2025 Continued To Jun-30-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT X CORP.'s Motion To Dismiss The Complaint For Forum Non Conveniens Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Stay.

Defendant X Corporation's Motion To Dismiss The Complaint For Forum Non Conveniens Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Stay is continued to June 30, 2025, to provide the parties time to file and the court to decide an application for complex designation under California Rules of Court 3.400. Upon preliminary review of the case and the instant motion, it appears the case may be appropriate for designation. It involves sixty-seven Plaintiffs, could require management of a large number of witnesses or a substantial amount of documentary evidence, and most importantly could require "coordination with related actions pending in one or more courts in other counties, states, or countries, or in federal court." (CRC 3.400(b)(4).) Similar claims are set to be decided in a federal class action transferred to Delaware from California District Court. (Cornet, et al. v. Twitter, Inc., Case No. 3:22-cv-06857-JD.) Another class action case filed in this court, which is designated complex, makes similar claims. (Rebekah Justice, et al. v. Twitter, Inc., CGC-23-604287.) That case is stayed pending resolution of the federal class action. It appears that this case could benefit from increased judicial management.

No later than June 20, 2025, the parties shall file a joint statement regarding application for complex designation under California Rules of Court 3.400 discussing their efforts and the status of any application.

Defendant X Corporation shall provide a courtesy copy of such filing to this department within one business day of submission for filing.

Defendant X Corporation shall submit a proposed order setting out the above text verbatim.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
